hooks.command: HOOK="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.claude/hooks/bash-guard.py"; if [ -f "$HOOK" ]; then python3 "$HOOK"; else exit 0; fi
hooks.description: All Bash guards: secrets, destructive, commits, deploy, migrations, deps

hooks.command: HOOK="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.claude/hooks/write-guard.py"; if [ -f "$HOOK" ]; then python3 "$HOOK"; else exit 0; fi
hooks.description: All Write/Edit guards: env, JWT, secrets, SHA pin, naming

hooks.command: HOOK="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.claude/hooks/post-write-guard.py"; if [ -f "$HOOK" ]; then python3 "$HOOK"; else exit 0; fi
hooks.description: All post-write guards: file size, BOM, console.log

hooks.prompt:
SESSION END — MANDATORY CHECKLIST.

1. UNCOMMITTED WORK: Are there uncommitted changes? If yes → /commit now.
2. DOCS: Were Blueprint/CDC/PET updated if code changed? If not → update now.
3. OBSIDIAN: Update 01-Projets/[project].md in Obsidian for every project touched this session (flat structure post 2026-04-11). Only write to files where this session produced changes — not all project files.
4. SESSION REPORT: Save to docs/Sessions/Session-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN.md
5. SCORING V2: Score 3 dimensions — Value (40%): deployable/publishable output? Reliability (30%): rework count, regressions? Process (30%): methodology gates respected? Formula: (Value × 0.4) + (Reliability × 0.3) + (Process × 0.3).

Output reminders for any incomplete items. Do NOT let the session end with unfinished business.
hooks.description: Ring 1: Session end full checklist + Obsidian sync + report

hooks.prompt:
CONTEXT COMPACTION IMMINENT. Before the conversation is compressed:

1. SAVE STATE: Use TodoWrite to capture your current task list (in-progress and pending items).
2. NOTE DECISIONS: If any important decisions were made this session that are NOT yet in a commit or file, write them to a brief comment in your next response.
3. NOTE FILES: List the files you have modified or are actively working on.
4. WARN JAY: Tell Jay that compaction is happening and what you are preserving.

This ensures continuity after compression. Do NOT skip this.
hooks.description: Save session state before context compaction

hooks.prompt:
CONTEXT WAS JUST COMPACTED. Critical recovery steps:

1. READ TODOS: Check your todo list — it contains your task state from before compaction.
2. RE-READ CLAUDE.md: Your identity rules are in .claude/CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/. They survive compaction but refresh your awareness.
3. CHECK GIT: Run 'git status' and 'git log --oneline -5' to see where you are.
4. TELL JAY: Briefly tell Jay what you remember working on and ask if anything is missing.
5. CONTINUE: Resume the in-progress task from your todo list.

Do NOT start new work without confirming continuity with Jay.
hooks.description: Restore session state after context compaction

hooks.prompt:
DEBUG ESCALATION CHECK. A Bash command just failed.

1. LOGS FIRST: Have the error logs been read? If hypothesizing without logs: 'RULE: LOGS FIRST. Read error output before hypothesizing.'
2. REPEATED FAILURE: Is this the 2nd+ failure on the same issue? If yes: 'WARNING: Multiple failures. Escalate: L1 (logs) → L2 (SKB + web 8 languages) → L3 (report to Jay).'
3. CONTEXT RESET: After 2 failed corrections → suggest /clear or new conversation.
